latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Elephants cool blood through giant ears
latest.script_key: elephants use their ears as living radiators watch the ear surface because warm blood flows through those broad veins moving air can dump heat before it spreads through the body that is why the ears are more than decoration in hot places which giant animal trick next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Dogs build a map with smell

latest.title: Why dog play is more serious than it looks right before the move
latest.script_key: dogs use play to practice timing trust and control watch the first second the body pauses then the first playful move gives the clue in this clip the recovery angle is a barking dog the movement looks random until you notice the setup that visible cue is the whole story so the short opens on the dog instead of stretching the setup this keeps the hook specific and the payoff immediate

latest.title: Why dog paws explain the move when the body freezes

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Giraffes stretch for survival not snacks
latest.script_key: long necks aren t for snacks they re for the last leaf notice those knobby horns males fight for the few branches that stay green all year that s why they stretch so high because the easy food ran out weeks ago so next time you see one towering over a tree ask yourself is it eating or just outlasting

latest.title: Snakes sample the air with a tongue flick
latest.script_key: snakes sample the air with a tongue flick watch the tongue flick because it collects scent particles and sends them to a special organ in the mouth that helps snakes track prey mates and danger without needing a loud chase the air is information would you notice the trail
